---
title: "density_food"
author: "Ruwen Zhou"
date: "9/2/2024"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(readr)
library(plyr)
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(readxl)
library(ggplot2)
library(phyloseq)
library(vegan)
library(DESeq2)
library(pheatmap)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(ggpubr)
```

```{r}
food_data = read_xlsx("data/FFQA862_new.xlsx")
nutrition_data = read_xlsx("data/FFQB800.xlsx")
metadata = read_xlsx("data/ethnicity_866.xlsx")

food_data[-1] <- lapply(food_data[-1], function(x) as.double(as.character(x)))
food_data <- food_data %>%
  mutate(Participant_ID = as.character(Participant_ID))
food_data
nutrition_data
metadata
```

```{r}
ethnicity_colors <- c("C" = "#9FDDCA", "I" = "#9EA3F1", "M" = "#E17887", "O" = "#80796BFF")

merged_data <- food_data %>%
  inner_join(metadata[,c("FREG1_Barcode", "FREG5_Ethnic_Group")], by = c("Participant_ID" = "FREG1_Barcode"))

# Filter out the 'O' ethnic group and select relevant columns
plot_data <- merged_data %>%
  filter(FREG5_Ethnic_Group != "O") %>%
  dplyr::select(FREG5_Ethnic_Group, everything(), -Participant_ID)

# Get the names of all food columns
food_columns <- names(plot_data)[names(plot_data) != "FREG5_Ethnic_Group"]
food_columns
merged_data
```


```{r}
# Create a directory to store the plots
dir.create("food_density_plots", showWarnings = FALSE)

# Loop through each food group and create individual density plots
for (food in food_columns) {
  p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = .data[[food]], fill = FREG5_Ethnic_Group)) +
    geom_density(alpha = 0.7) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = ethnicity_colors) +
    labs(title = paste(food),
         x = "Consumption",
         y = "Density",
         fill = "Ethnic Group") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(legend.position = "bottom",
          plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"),
          axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
          axis.text = element_text(size = 10),
          legend.title = element_text(size = 12),
          legend.text = element_text(size = 10))
  
  # Save the plot
  # ggsave(paste0("figure/food_density_plots/", food, "_density_plot.png"), 
  #        plot = p, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)
  
  # Print progress
  cat("Saved plot for", food, "\n")
}

cat("All plots have been saved in the 'food_density_plots' directory.\n")
```

```{r}
library(rstatix)
stat_results <- data.frame(Food = character(),
                           KW_statistic = numeric(),
                           KW_p_value = numeric(),
                           KW_p_adjusted = numeric(),
                           Significant_pairs = character(),
                           stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# Loop through each food group
for (food in food_columns) {
  # Kruskal-Wallis test
  kw_test <- plot_data %>%
    kruskal_test(as.formula(paste(food, "~ FREG5_Ethnic_Group")))
  
  # Store results (without adjusted p-value for now)
  stat_results <- stat_results %>%
    add_row(Food = food,
            KW_statistic = kw_test$statistic,
            KW_p_value = kw_test$p,
            KW_p_adjusted = NA,
            Significant_pairs = "")
  
  # Print progress
  cat("Processed", food, "\n")
}

# Adjust p-values using Benjamini-Hochberg method
stat_results$KW_p_adjusted <- p.adjust(stat_results$KW_p_value, method = "BH")

# Perform Dunn's test for foods with significant adjusted p-values
for (i in 1:nrow(stat_results)) {
  if (stat_results$KW_p_adjusted[i] < 0.05) {
    food <- stat_results$Food[i]
    dunn_test <- plot_data %>%
      dunn_test(as.formula(paste(food, "~ FREG5_Ethnic_Group")), p.adjust.method = "bonferroni")
    
    # Get significant pairs
    sig_pairs <- dunn_test %>%
      filter(p.adj < 0.05) %>%
      mutate(pair = paste(group1, group2, sep="-")) %>%
      pull(pair) %>%
      paste(collapse = ", ")
    
    stat_results$Significant_pairs[i] <- ifelse(sig_pairs == "", "None", sig_pairs)
  } else {
    stat_results$Significant_pairs[i] <- "None"
  }
}

# Save statistical results
#write.csv(stat_results, "figure/food_statistical_results.csv", row.names = FALSE)

cat("Statistical results have been saved in 'food_statistical_results.csv'.\n")

# Print summary of significant differences
cat("\nSummary of significant differences (after FDR correction):\n")
stat_results %>%
  filter(KW_p_adjusted < 0.05) %>%
  select(Food, KW_p_adjusted, Significant_pairs) %>%
  arrange(KW_p_adjusted) %>%
  print(n = Inf)

# Print foods with no significant differences
cat("\nFoods with no significant differences among ethnic groups (after FDR correction):\n")
stat_results %>%
  filter(KW_p_adjusted >= 0.05) %>%
  pull(Food) %>%
  cat(sep = ", ")
```
## boxplot
```{r}
library(reshape2)


merged_data <- merge(food_data, metadata[,c("FREG1_Barcode", "FREG5_Ethnic_Group")], by.x = "Participant_ID", by.y = "FREG1_Barcode")

# Filter the ethnic groups C, I, M only
filtered_data <- merged_data %>% filter(FREG5_Ethnic_Group %in% c("C", "I", "M"))

# Reshape the data into long format for ggplot
long_data <- melt(filtered_data, id.vars = c("Participant_ID", "FREG5_Ethnic_Group"), 
                  variable.name = "Food_Group", value.name = "Intake")


f = ggplot(long_data, aes(x = FREG5_Ethnic_Group, y = Intake, fill = FREG5_Ethnic_Group)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  facet_wrap(~Food_Group, scales = "free_y") +  # facet_wrap to create separate plots, free_y for different y-axis ranges
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1)) + 
  labs(title = "Box Plots of Food Intake by Ethnic Group for Each Food Group",
       x = "Ethnic Group", y = "Intake") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = ethnicity_colors)
#ggsave("figure/boxplot_raw.png", plot = f, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)
f
```

```{r}

remove_top_bottom_1_percent <- function(df) {
  df %>%
    group_by(Food_Group) %>%
    dplyr::filter(Intake >= quantile(Intake, 0.05) & Intake <= quantile(Intake, 0.95))
}

# Apply the function to remove the top and bottom 1%
long_data_filtered <- remove_top_bottom_1_percent(long_data)

# Create separate boxplots for each food group without top 1% and bottom 1%
t = ggplot(long_data_filtered, aes(x = FREG5_Ethnic_Group, y = Intake, fill = FREG5_Ethnic_Group)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  facet_wrap(~Food_Group, scales = "free_y") +  # facet_wrap to create separate plots, free_y for different y-axis ranges
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1)) + 
  labs(title = "Box Plots of Food Intake by Ethnic Group for Each Food Group (remove outlier)",
       x = "Ethnic Group", y = "Intake") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = ethnicity_colors)

# ggsave("figure/boxplot_raw_no_outlier2.png", plot = t, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)
t
```

```{r}
significant_pairs_list <- list(
  "Seafood" = list(c("C", "I"), c("I", "M")),
  "Poultry" = list(c("C", "I"), c("I", "M")),
  "Red_meat" = list(c("C", "I"), c("C", "M"), c("I", "M")),
  "Dairy" = list(c("C", "I")),
  "Cruciferous_veg" = list(c("C", "M")),
  "Legumes" = list(c("C", "I"), c("I", "M")),
  "Mushrooms" = list(c("C", "I"), c("C", "M")),
  "Fruits" = NULL,
  "Chapati" = list(c("C", "I"), c("I", "M")),
  "Idli_Thosai" = list(c("C", "I"), c("C", "M"), c("I", "M")),
  "Noodle_dishes" = list(c("C", "I"), c("I", "M")),
  "Coffee" = NULL,
  "Tea" = NULL,
  "Longtong" = list(c("C", "M"), c("I", "M")),
  "Pork" = list(c("C", "I"), c("C", "M")),
  "Rice_dishes" = NULL,
  "Bakery_bread" = list(c("C", "I")),
  "Leafy_veg" = list(c("C", "I"), c("C", "M")),
  "Root_veg" = NULL,
  "Eggs" = list(c("C", "I"))
)

```

```{r  fig.height=6, fig.width=6}
p <- ggplot(long_data_filtered, aes(x = FREG5_Ethnic_Group, y = Intake, fill = FREG5_Ethnic_Group)) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.5, outlier.size = 0.5) +
  facet_wrap(~Food_Group, scales = "free_y", ncol = 4) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 8) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 6),
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 8),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold")
  ) + 
  labs(title = "Box Plots of Food Intake by Ethnic Group with Significance",
       y = "Intake") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = ethnicity_colors)

p <- p + stat_compare_means(
  aes(label = ..p.signif..), 
  method = "kruskal.test", 
  label.y = function(x) max(x) + 0.1 * diff(range(x)),
  hide.ns = TRUE,
  size = 3
)
# Add pairwise comparisons
for (food_group in names(significant_pairs_list)) {
  if (!is.null(significant_pairs_list[[food_group]])) {
    p <- p + geom_signif(
      data = long_data_filtered %>% filter(Food_Group == food_group),
      comparisons = significant_pairs_list[[food_group]],
      step_increase = 0.1,
      map_signif_level = TRUE,
      test = "wilcox.test",
      textsize = 2,
      tip_length = 0.01
    )
  }
}

# Add significance annotations
# p <- p + stat_compare_means(
#   aes(label = ..p.signif..), 
#   method = "kruskal.test", 
#   hide.ns = TRUE,
#   size = 3
# )

# Add pairwise comparisons
# for (food_group in names(significant_pairs_list)) {
#   if (!is.null(significant_pairs_list[[food_group]])) {
#     p <- p + geom_signif(
#       data = long_data_filtered %>% filter(Food_Group == food_group),
#       comparisons = significant_pairs_list[[food_group]],
#       step_increase = 0.1,
#       map_signif_level = TRUE,
#       test = "wilcox.test",
#       textsize = 2,
#       tip_length = 0.01
#     )
#   }
# }

# Print the plot
print(p)
#ggsave("figure/figure3/boxplot_no_outlier_sig3.pdf", plot = p, width = 10, height = 10)
```

## ternary diagrams
```{r}
library(ggtern)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
```


```{r}
food_columns <- c("Poultry", "Red_meat", "Dairy", "Cruciferous_veg", "Legumes", 
                  "Mushrooms", "Fruits", "Chapati", "Idli_Thosai", "Noodle_dishes", 
                  "Coffee", "Tea", "Lontong", "Pork", "Seafood", "Rice_dishes", 
                  "Bakery_bread", "Leafy_veg", "Root_veg", "Eggs")

# Step 2: Calculate per-capita mean intake for each food by ethnic group
ternary_data <- merged_data %>%
  group_by(FREG5_Ethnic_Group) %>%
  summarise(dplyr::across(all_of(food_columns), ~mean(.x, na.rm = TRUE))) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = all_of(food_columns), 
               names_to = "Food", 
               values_to = "Mean_Intake")

# Step 3: Reshape to wide format for ternary calculation
ternary_wide <- ternary_data %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = FREG5_Ethnic_Group, 
              values_from = Mean_Intake)

# Step 4: Calculate proportions for ternary diagram
# Ethnic groups are: C (Chinese), I (Indian), M (Malay)
ternary_wide <- ternary_wide %>%
  mutate(
    Total = C + I + M,
    C_prop = C / Total,
    I_prop = I / Total,
    M_prop = M / Total
  )

# Step 5: Create ternary diagram with smart text positioning
library(ggrepel)  # For non-overlapping labels

# Add a column to determine text position based on location
ternary_wide <- ternary_wide %>%
  mutate(
    hjust_val = case_when(
      M_prop > 0.6 ~ 1,  # Right side of plot, text on left
      C_prop > 0.6 ~ 0,  # Left side of plot, text on right
      TRUE ~ 0.5         # Middle, text centered
    )
  )

p1 <- ggtern(data = ternary_wide, 
       aes(x = C_prop, y = I_prop, z = M_prop)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.7, color = "steelblue") +
  geom_text(aes(label = Food, hjust = hjust_val), size = 2.5, vjust = 0) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme_showarrows() +
  theme(tern.plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
        plot.margin = unit(c(1, 3, 1, 3), "cm")) +
  labs(title = "Ternary Diagram of Food Intake by Ethnic Group",
       subtitle = "Per-capita consumption patterns",
       x = "Chinese (C)",
       y = "Indian (I)",
       z = "Malay (M)")

print(p1)

# Expand the plot window in RStudio, or save to file
# ggsave("figure/ternary_food_intake.pdf", plot = p1, width = 12, height = 10, dpi = 300)
```


```{r}
ternary_wide <- ternary_wide %>%
  mutate(Category = case_when(
    Food %in% c("Poultry", "Red_meat", "Pork", "Seafood", "Eggs") ~ "Protein",
    Food %in% c("Dairy") ~ "Dairy",
    Food %in% c("Cruciferous_veg", "Leafy_veg", "Root_veg") ~ "Vegetables",
    Food %in% c("Legumes", "Mushrooms") ~ "Plant-based",
    Food %in% c("Fruits") ~ "Fruits",
    Food %in% c("Chapati", "Idli_Thosai", "Noodle_dishes", "Lontong", 
                "Rice_dishes", "Bakery_bread") ~ "Carbohydrates",
    Food %in% c("Coffee", "Tea") ~ "Beverages",
    TRUE ~ "Other"
  ))

# Colored version with smart text positioning
p2 <- ggtern(data = ternary_wide, 
       aes(x = C_prop, y = I_prop, z = M_prop, color = Category)) +
  geom_point(size = 4, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = Food, hjust = hjust_val), size = 2.5, vjust = 0, 
            show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme_showarrows() +
  theme(tern.plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
        plot.margin = unit(c(1, 3, 1, 3), "cm")) +
  labs(title = "Ternary Diagram of Food Intake by Ethnic Group",
       subtitle = "Per-capita consumption patterns colored by food category",
       x = "Chinese (C)",
       y = "Indian (I)",
       z = "Malay (M)")

print(p2)

# Step 6: View the data table
print(ternary_wide)
#ggsave("figure/ternary_food_intake_color.pdf", plot = p2, width = 12, height = 10, dpi = 300)
```

"data/filtered_species602.xlsx"
```{r}
# Step 1: Read species data
species_data <- read_excel("data/filtered_species602.xlsx")

# Step 2: Read metadata
# Assuming you have metadata with FREG1_Barcode and FREG5_Ethnic_Group
metadata_subset <- metadata[, c("FREG1_Barcode", "FREG5_Ethnic_Group")]

# Step 3: Merge species data with metadata
species_merged <- species_data %>%
  left_join(metadata_subset, by = "FREG1_Barcode")

# Step 4: Get all species column names (excluding FREG1_Barcode)
species_columns <- setdiff(colnames(species_data), "FREG1_Barcode")

# Check the structure
cat("Total number of species columns:", length(species_columns), "\n")
cat("First few species names:\n")
print(head(species_columns))

# Step 5: Extract genus from species names (first word)
# For "Bradyrhizobium diazoefficiens" -> "Bradyrhizobium"
genus_mapping <- data.frame(
  Species = species_columns,
  Genus = sapply(strsplit(species_columns, " "), function(x) x[1]),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Step 6: Expanded manual mapping for common bacterial classes
# Mapping genus to class (higher taxonomic level = fewer colors)
genus_to_taxonomy <- data.frame(
  Genus = c(
    # Clostridia (Firmicutes)
    "Faecalibacterium", "Ruminococcus", "Clostridium", "Roseburia", "Blautia",
    "Coprococcus", "Dorea", "Eubacterium", "Veillonella", "Megasphaera", "Dialister",
    "Anaerostipes", "Oscillibacter", "Subdoligranulum",
    # Bacilli (Firmicutes)
    "Lactobacillus", "Streptococcus", "Enterococcus", "Staphylococcus", "Bacillus",
    "Lactococcus", "Leuconostoc",
    # Bacteroidia (Bacteroidetes)
    "Bacteroides", "Prevotella", "Parabacteroides", "Alistipes", "Barnesiella",
    "Odoribacter", "Butyricimonas",
    # Actinobacteria
    "Bifidobacterium", "Collinsella", "Eggerthella", "Actinomyces",
    "Mycobacterium", "Corynebacterium", "Nocardia", "Streptomyces",
    "Propionibacterium", "Cutibacterium",
    # Gammaproteobacteria
    "Escherichia", "Klebsiella", "Enterobacter", "Citrobacter", "Salmonella",
    "Shigella", "Proteus", "Serratia", "Yersinia",
    "Pseudomonas", "Acinetobacter", "Stenotrophomonas", "Vibrio",
    "Haemophilus", "Pasteurella",
    # Alphaproteobacteria
    "Rhizobium", "Sinorhizobium", "Mesorhizobium", "Bradyrhizobium",
    "Agrobacterium", "Ensifer", "Neorhizobium", "Starkeya",
    "Sphingomonas", "Caulobacter", "Bartonella",
    # Betaproteobacteria
    "Burkholderia", "Neisseria", "Sutterella", "Oxalobacter",
    "Ralstonia", "Herbaspirillum",
    # Deltaproteobacteria
    "Desulfovibrio", "Bilophila",
    # Epsilonproteobacteria
    "Campylobacter", "Helicobacter", "Arcobacter",
    # Verrucomicrobiae
    "Akkermansia",
    # Fusobacteriia
    "Fusobacterium", "Leptotrichia",
    # Negativicutes (Firmicutes)
    "Selenomonas", "Sporomusa"
  ),
  Class = c(
    # Clostridia
    "Clostridia", "Clostridia", "Clostridia", "Clostridia", "Clostridia",
    "Clostridia", "Clostridia", "Clostridia", "Negativicutes", "Negativicutes", "Negativicutes",
    "Clostridia", "Clostridia", "Clostridia",
    # Bacilli
    "Bacilli", "Bacilli", "Bacilli", "Bacilli", "Bacilli",
    "Bacilli", "Bacilli",
    # Bacteroidia
    "Bacteroidia", "Bacteroidia", "Bacteroidia", "Bacteroidia", "Bacteroidia",
    "Bacteroidia", "Bacteroidia",
    # Actinobacteria
    "Actinobacteria", "Actinobacteria", "Actinobacteria", "Actinobacteria",
    "Actinobacteria", "Actinobacteria", "Actinobacteria", "Actinobacteria",
    "Actinobacteria", "Actinobacteria",
    # Gammaproteobacteria
    "Gammaproteobacteria", "Gammaproteobacteria", "Gammaproteobacteria", "Gammaproteobacteria", "Gammaproteobacteria",
    "Gammaproteobacteria", "Gammaproteobacteria", "Gammaproteobacteria", "Gammaproteobacteria",
    "Gammaproteobacteria", "Gammaproteobacteria", "Gammaproteobacteria", "Gammaproteobacteria",
    "Gammaproteobacteria", "Gammaproteobacteria",
    # Alphaproteobacteria
    "Alphaproteobacteria", "Alphaproteobacteria", "Alphaproteobacteria", "Alphaproteobacteria",
    "Alphaproteobacteria", "Alphaproteobacteria", "Alphaproteobacteria", "Alphaproteobacteria",
    "Alphaproteobacteria", "Alphaproteobacteria", "Alphaproteobacteria",
    # Betaproteobacteria
    "Betaproteobacteria", "Betaproteobacteria", "Betaproteobacteria", "Betaproteobacteria",
    "Betaproteobacteria", "Betaproteobacteria",
    # Deltaproteobacteria
    "Deltaproteobacteria", "Deltaproteobacteria",
    # Epsilonproteobacteria
    "Epsilonproteobacteria", "Epsilonproteobacteria", "Epsilonproteobacteria",
    # Verrucomicrobiae
    "Verrucomicrobiae",
    # Fusobacteriia
    "Fusobacteriia", "Fusobacteriia",
    # Negativicutes
    "Negativicutes", "Negativicutes"
  ),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Merge genus with class
genus_mapping <- genus_mapping %>%
  left_join(genus_to_taxonomy, by = "Genus")
```


```{r}
# Check how many genera we could map
cat("\nGenus mapping summary:\n")
cat("Total genera:", length(unique(genus_mapping$Genus)), "\n")
cat("Mapped to class:", sum(!is.na(genus_mapping$Class)), "\n")
cat("Unmapped:", sum(is.na(genus_mapping$Class)), "\n")

# Step 7: Calculate per-capita mean abundance for each species by ethnic group
ternary_species <- species_merged %>%
  group_by(FREG5_Ethnic_Group) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(across(all_of(species_columns), ~ mean(.x, na.rm = TRUE)))%>%
  pivot_longer(cols = all_of(species_columns), 
               names_to = "Species", 
               values_to = "Mean_Abundance")

# Step 8: Reshape to wide format for ternary calculation
ternary_wide_species <- ternary_species %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = FREG5_Ethnic_Group, 
              values_from = Mean_Abundance)

# Step 9: Add class information and FILTER OUT unmapped species
ternary_wide_species <- ternary_wide_species %>%
  left_join(genus_mapping, by = "Species") %>%
  filter(!is.na(Class))  # Remove species without class mapping

cat("\nAfter filtering unmapped species:\n")
cat("Species retained:", nrow(ternary_wide_species), "\n")

# Step 10: Calculate proportions for ternary diagram
ternary_wide_species <- ternary_wide_species %>%
  mutate(
    Total = C + I + M,
    C_prop = C / Total,
    I_prop = I / Total,
    M_prop = M / Total
  )

# Step 11: Remove species with zero or NA values
ternary_wide_species <- ternary_wide_species %>%
  filter(!is.na(Total) & Total > 0)
ternary_wide_species <- ternary_wide_species %>%
  mutate(
    point_size = case_when(
      C_prop > 0.7 | I_prop > 0.7 | M_prop > 0.7 ~ 6,  # Large for corner points
      C_prop > 0.5 | I_prop > 0.5 | M_prop > 0.5 ~ 4,  # Medium for near-corner
      TRUE ~ 3                                          # Small for center points
    )
  )

# Step 12: Use Class instead of Family for coloring
# This will give us ~10-12 colors instead of 30+

# Convert to regular dataframe if it's a tibble with list columns
ternary_wide_species <- as.data.frame(ternary_wide_species)


# Count classes
class_counts <- ternary_wide_species %>%
  group_by(Class) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(n = n(), .groups = 'drop') %>%
  arrange(dplyr::desc(n))

cat("\nClass distribution:\n")
print(class_counts)

# No need to group "Other" since classes are already at a high level
ternary_wide_species$Class_display <- ternary_wide_species$Class
```


```{r}
library(RColorBrewer)

n_classes <- length(unique(ternary_wide_species$Class_display))
cat("\nNumber of classes to display:", n_classes, "\n")


# color_palette <- brewer.pal(min(12, n_classes), "Paired")

# color_palette <- brewer.pal(min(8, n_classes), "Dark2")


# if(n_classes <= 8) {
#   color_palette <- brewer.pal(n_classes, "Dark2")
# } else if(n_classes <= 12) {
#   color_palette <- brewer.pal(n_classes, "Paired")
# } else {
#   # For more than 12 classes, combine multiple palettes
#   color_palette <- c(
#     brewer.pal(8, "Dark2"),
#     brewer.pal(8, "Set2"),
#     brewer.pal(max(n_classes - 16, 3), "Set3")
#   )[1:n_classes]
# }

color_palette <- c(
  "#E41A1C", "#377EB8", "#4DAF4A", "#984EA3", "#FF7F00",
  "#FFFF33", "#A65628", "#F781BF", "#66C2A5", "#FC8D62",
  "#8DA0CB", "#E5C494")[1:n_classes]
```


```{r}
p_microbiome <- ggtern(data = ternary_wide_species, 
       aes(x = C_prop, y = I_prop, z = M_prop, color = Class_display)) +
  geom_point(aes(size = point_size), alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_size_identity() +
  scale_color_manual(values = color_palette) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme_showarrows() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    legend.text = element_text(size = 9),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold"),
    tern.plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    plot.margin = unit(c(0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5), "cm")
  ) +
  guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 4, alpha = 1))) +
  labs(
    title = "Ternary Diagram of Gut Microbiome Species by Ethnic Group",
    subtitle = "Per-capita abundance patterns colored by bacterial class",
    x = "Chinese (C)",
    y = "Indian (I)",
    z = "Malay (M)",
    color = "Bacterial Class"
  )

print(p_microbiome)
```


```{r}

#ggsave("figure/ternary_microbiome_all3.pdf", plot = p_microbiome,
       #width = 12, height = 10, dpi = 300)
# ggsave("figure/ternary_microbiome_all2.png", plot = p_microbiome_labeled,
#        width = 12, height = 10, dpi = 300)
ternary_wide_species
#ternary_wide_species$Species
# write.csv(ternary_wide_species, "ternary_species_data.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```


```{r}
# Identify foods at each corner (angle)
# Chinese corner (high C_prop)
chinese_species <- ternary_wide_species %>%
  filter(C_prop > 0.7) %>%
  arrange(dplyr::desc(C_prop)) %>%
  dplyr::select(Species, Class, C_prop, I_prop, M_prop)

cat("\n=== CHINESE CORNER (C_prop > 0.7) ===\n")
print(chinese_species, n = Inf)

# Indian corner (high I_prop)
indian_species <- ternary_wide_species %>%
  filter(I_prop > 0.5) %>%
  arrange(dplyr::desc(I_prop)) %>%
  dplyr::select(Species, Class, C_prop, I_prop, M_prop)

cat("\n=== INDIAN CORNER (I_prop > 0.7) ===\n")
print(indian_species, n = Inf)

# Malay corner (high M_prop)
malay_species <- ternary_wide_species %>%
  filter(M_prop > 0.7) %>%
  arrange(dplyr::desc(M_prop)) %>%
  dplyr::select(Species, Class, C_prop, I_prop, M_prop)

cat("\n=== MALAY CORNER (M_prop > 0.7) ===\n")
print(malay_species, n = Inf)


indian_species
# Summary count
cat("\n=== SUMMARY ===\n")
cat("species at Chinese corner:", nrow(chinese_species), "\n")
cat("species at Indian corner:", nrow(indian_species), "\n")
cat("species at Malay corner:", nrow(malay_species), "\n")
```


## all food
```{r}
food_data_all <- read_excel("data/all_food_863.xlsx")
food_data_all
```


```{r}
# Install required packages if not already installed
# install.packages(c("ggtern", "dplyr", "tidyr"))

library(ggtern)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

# Step 1: Prepare metadata
metadata_subset <- metadata[, c("FREG1_Barcode", "FREG5_Ethnic_Group")]

# Step 2: Merge food data with metadata
# The first column is "participant_id"
food_merged <- food_data_all %>%
  left_join(metadata_subset, by = c("participant_id" = "FREG1_Barcode"))

# Step 3: Get all food column names (excluding participant_id, freg0_pid, and FREG5_Ethnic_Group)
food_columns <- setdiff(colnames(food_data_all), c("participant_id", "freg0_pid"))

cat("Total number of food items:", length(food_columns), "\n")
cat("First few food items:\n")
print(head(food_columns, 10))

# Step 4: Calculate per-capita mean intake for each food by ethnic group
ternary_food <- food_merged %>%
  group_by(FREG5_Ethnic_Group) %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(food_columns), ~mean(.x, na.rm = TRUE))) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = all_of(food_columns), 
               names_to = "Food", 
               values_to = "Mean_Intake")

# Step 5: Reshape to wide format for ternary calculation
ternary_wide_food <- ternary_food %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = FREG5_Ethnic_Group, 
              values_from = Mean_Intake)

# Step 6: Calculate proportions for ternary diagram
ternary_wide_food <- ternary_wide_food %>%
  mutate(
    Total = C + I + M,
    C_prop = C / Total,
    I_prop = I / Total,
    M_prop = M / Total
  )

# Step 7: Remove foods with zero or NA values
ternary_wide_food <- ternary_wide_food %>%
  filter(!is.na(Total) & Total > 0)
ternary_wide_food <- ternary_wide_food %>%
  mutate(
    point_size = case_when(
      C_prop > 0.7 | I_prop > 0.7 | M_prop > 0.7 ~ 6,  # Large for corner points
      C_prop > 0.5 | I_prop > 0.5 | M_prop > 0.5 ~ 4,  # Medium for near-corner
      TRUE ~ 3                                          # Small for center points
    )
  )

cat("\nFoods retained after filtering:", nrow(ternary_wide_food), "\n")

# Step 8: Create food categories based on your 20 defined food groups
# Foods that don't match any category will be removed
ternary_wide_food <- ternary_wide_food %>%
  mutate(Category = case_when(
    # Poultry
    grepl("poultry|chicken", Food) ~ "Protein",
    
    # Red_meat (beef, mutton, organs, but NOT pork)
    grepl("beef|mutton|burger|organs|^meat_", Food) & !grepl("pork|egg_mix", Food) ~ "Protein",
    
    # Pork (separate from red meat)
    grepl("pork|cured_pork", Food) ~ "Protein",
    
    # Seafood
    grepl("fish|seafood|prawn|crab|squid|oyster|ngiri", Food) ~ "Protein",
    
    # Dairy
    grepl("^milk_|^yoghurt_|^cheese_|^malted_drink|milkshake|yoghurt_drink", Food) ~ "Dairy",
    
    # Eggs
    grepl("egg", Food) ~ "Protein",
    
    # Cruciferous_veg (broccoli, cabbage, cauliflower)
    grepl("broccoli|cabbage|cauliflower", Food) ~ "Plant-based",
    
    # Leafy_veg
    grepl("chye_sim|kailan|kang_kong|spinach|lettuce", Food) ~ "Plant-based",
    
    # Root_veg
    grepl("carrot|potato|yam|lotus_root|pumpkin", Food) ~ "Plant-based",
    
    # Legumes (includes beans, peas, dhal, tofu, beancurd)
    grepl("dhal|peas|beans|tofu|beancurd", Food) ~ "Plant-based",
    
    # Mushrooms
    grepl("mushroom", Food) ~ "Plant-based",
    
    # Fruits
    grepl("apple|avocado|banana|blueberr|dragonfruit|durian|grape|guava|kiwi|mango|melon|orange|papaya|pear|pineapple|strawberr|dried_fruit|fruit_juice", Food) ~ "Fruits",
    
    # Rice_dishes
    grepl("rice|^plain_porr|^flav_porr|^flav_rice|^glut_rice|rice_roll", Food) ~ "Carbohydrates",
    
    # Noodle_dishes
    grepl("noodle|pasta", Food) ~ "Carbohydrates",
    
    # Chapati (Indian flatbreads - chapati, roti, puri, murtabak)
    grepl("chapati|^roti$|puri|murtabak", Food) ~ "Carbohydrates",
    
    # Idli_Thosai
    grepl("thosai|idli", Food) ~ "Carbohydrates",
    
    # Lontong
    grepl("lontong", Food) ~ "Carbohydrates",
    
    # Bakery_bread (breads, buns, toast, pizza)
    grepl("bread|toast|pizza|buns|french_toast", Food) ~ "Carbohydrates",
    
    # Coffee
    grepl("coffee|bottled_coffee", Food) ~ "Beverages",
    
    # Tea
    grepl("^tea_|bottled_tea", Food) ~ "Beverages",
    
    TRUE ~ NA_character_  # Mark as NA for removal
  )) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Category))  # Remove foods that don't belong to any of the 20 categories

cat("\nFoods retained after filtering to 20 categories:", nrow(ternary_wide_food), "\n")
cat("\nFood category distribution:\n")
category_table <- table(ternary_wide_food$Category)
print(category_table[order(-category_table)])

# Step 9: Create ternary diagram without labels (too many foods to label all)
library(RColorBrewer)

# n_categories <- length(unique(ternary_wide_food$Category))
# if(n_categories <= 8) {
#   color_palette <- brewer.pal(n_categories, "Dark2")
# } else if(n_categories <= 12) {
#   color_palette <- brewer.pal(n_categories, "Paired")
# } else {
#   color_palette <- c(
#     brewer.pal(8, "Dark2"),
#     brewer.pal(8, "Set2")
#   )[1:n_categories]
# }
color_palette_20 <- c(
  "#E41A1C",  # Red - Poultry
  "#377EB8",  # Blue - Red_meat
  "#4DAF4A",  # Green - Dairy
  "#984EA3",  # Purple - Cruciferous_veg
  "#FF7F00",  # Orange - Legumes
  "#FFFF33",  # Yellow - Mushrooms
  "#A65628",  # Brown - Fruits
  "#F781BF",  # Pink - Chapati
  "#66C2A5",  # Teal - Idli_Thosai
  "#FC8D62",  # Coral - Noodle_dishes
  "#8DA0CB",  # Lavender - Coffee
  "#E78AC3",  # Rose - Tea
  "#A6D854",  # Lime - Lontong
  "#FFD92F",  # Gold - Pork
  "#E5C494",  # Tan - Seafood
  "#B3B3B3",  # Gray - Rice_dishes
  "#1B9E77",  # Dark teal - Bakery_bread
  "#D95F02",  # Dark orange - Leafy_veg
  "#7570B3",  # Dark purple - Root_veg
  "#E7298A"   # Magenta - Eggs
)[1:9]

p_food <- ggtern(data = ternary_wide_food, 
       aes(x = C_prop, y = I_prop, z = M_prop, color = Category)) +
  geom_point(aes(size = point_size), alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_size_identity() +
  #scale_color_manual(values = color_palette_20) +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme_showarrows() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    legend.text = element_text(size = 9),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold"),
    tern.plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    plot.margin = unit(c(0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5), "cm")
  ) +
  guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 4, alpha = 1))) +
  labs(
    title = "Ternary Diagram of Food Intake by Ethnic Group",
    subtitle = "Per-capita consumption patterns across 248 food items",
    x = "Chinese (C)",
    y = "Indian (I)",
    z = "Malay (M)",
    color = "Food Category"
  )

print(p_food)

# ggsave("figure/ternary_food_item_large2.pdf", plot = p_food,
#        width = 12, height = 10, dpi = 300)
```


```{r}
# Identify foods at each corner (angle)
# Chinese corner (high C_prop)
chinese_foods <- ternary_wide_food %>%
  filter(C_prop > 0.7) %>%
  arrange(desc(C_prop)) %>%
  dplyr::select(Food, Category, C_prop, I_prop, M_prop)

cat("\n=== CHINESE CORNER (C_prop > 0.7) ===\n")
print(chinese_foods, n = Inf)

# Indian corner (high I_prop)
indian_foods <- ternary_wide_food %>%
  filter(I_prop > 0.7) %>%
  arrange(desc(I_prop)) %>%
  dplyr::select(Food, Category, C_prop, I_prop, M_prop)

cat("\n=== INDIAN CORNER (I_prop > 0.7) ===\n")
print(indian_foods, n = Inf)

# Malay corner (high M_prop)
malay_foods <- ternary_wide_food %>%
  filter(M_prop > 0.7) %>%
  arrange(desc(M_prop)) %>%
  dplyr::select(Food, Category, C_prop, I_prop, M_prop)

cat("\n=== MALAY CORNER (M_prop > 0.7) ===\n")
print(malay_foods, n = Inf)

# Optional: Export to CSV for easier viewing
# write.csv(chinese_foods, "corner_foods_chinese.csv", row.names = FALSE)
# write.csv(indian_foods, "corner_foods_indian.csv", row.names = FALSE)
# write.csv(malay_foods, "corner_foods_malay.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# Summary count
cat("\n=== SUMMARY ===\n")
cat("Foods at Chinese corner:", nrow(chinese_foods), "\n")
cat("Foods at Indian corner:", nrow(indian_foods), "\n")
cat("Foods at Malay corner:", nrow(malay_foods), "\n")
```
